chains.name: XSS → Session Hijack → Full Account Takeover
steps: 1. Inject DOM XSS payload into Juice Shop search
2. Steal victim's JWT token from localStorage
3. Use stolen token to access /rest/user/whoami
4. Change victim's password, lock them out
5. Full account takeover confirmed
chains.real_world: Same technique used in GitHub Gist XSS attack (2023)

chains.name: SQLi → User Dump → Admin Login → Server Takeover
steps: 1. SQL injection on /rest/products/search to dump Users table
2. Extract admin email and password hash
3. Login as admin with stolen credentials
4. Access /rest/admin/application-configuration
5. Full administrative control achieved
chains.real_world: Same technique used in Equifax breach (2017)

chains.name: SSRF → Internal Network → Cloud Metadata → Credential Theft
steps: 1. SSRF via search endpoint to access internal services
2. Hit cloud metadata endpoint (169.254.169.254)
3. Extract IAM credentials from metadata
4. Use credentials to access cloud resources
5. Full cloud infrastructure compromise
chains.real_world: Same technique used in Capital One breach (2019)

chains.name: JWT Forgery → Admin API Access → Data Exfiltration
steps: 1. Forge JWT with alg=none and admin role
2. Use forged JWT to access /api/Users
3. Enumerate all users via IDOR on basket endpoints
4. Export user data via /rest/user/data-export
5. Complete user database exfiltrated

chains.name: NoSQL Injection → Data Manipulation → Race Condition → Financial Loss
steps: 1. NoSQL injection to manipulate product reviews
2. Exploit coupon race condition for unlimited discounts
3. Place orders with manipulated prices
4. Financial loss to the business confirmed
chains.real_world: Similar to Starbucks coupon race condition (2019)
